title: Beach crest spacing fix
summary: The first beach spatial-extension pass lands well overall, but the new dune crest currently sits inside the beach map-return interaction radius, so the reward beat reads as a travel post instead of a scenic sandy rise. This follow-up separates the crest from the map-return label without disturbing the inland corridor, lee-pocket, or tidepool approach.

execution_order.goal: Separate the dune crest from the beach map-return interaction radius.
execution_order.why_now: The current crest reward is visually overridden by the map-return label, so the beach extension does not yet read as three distinct movement beats.
The follow-up should stay narrow and preserve the rest of the beach extension once the travel-prompt conflict is resolved.

guardrails: Keep the beach inside the current world size, height, and camera band.
Do not add a new travel system, cue language, or traversal mechanic.
Keep the inland corridor door as the first obvious left-side travel anchor.
Preserve the lee-pocket and tidepool-approach families unless a tiny adjustment is required for readability.
The dune crest proof state should no longer show an in-range travel target.
